summary: This bundle records deterministic proof for four remaining proof-debt claims: persona_prompt product-owned loop boundaries, blocked review reason-code packets, runner assessment commit-drift provenance, and active claim discovery excludes for archived/superseded claim docs.

evidenceScope.targetSurface: deterministic Cautilus live-run invocation, review-variant reporting, runner-readiness, and claim-discovery scan-scope tests
evidenceScope.claimBoundary: Only the four listed behavior claims are marked satisfied. The named-adapter compare-report persistence claim remains unresolved because existing product tests connect already-persisted report artifacts to review packets but do not prove eval-test commands themselves persist compare-report.md/json files.
notClaimed: Every host-owned adapter command persists compare-report.md and compare-report.json
Cautilus owns generating compare reports for all named adapters
Persona prompt behavior quality is evaluated by these deterministic tests
Commit drift is ignored when adapter or runner file hashes differ
Archived docs outside the configured claim discovery graph are globally unreachable for all tools

protectedBehaviors: persona_prompt live runs write product-owned request, simulator, turn, transcript, and result packets while delegating backend choice through adapter-owned command templates
blocked review variants preserve machine-readable reason codes and concrete reasons in variant and summary packets
unavailable executor failures classify as blocked with unavailable_executor and preserve successful variant outputs
claim discovery honors adapter excludes for archived spec docs, superseded claim catalogs, and maintainer-only linked Markdown
runner readiness exposes commit drift as fresh-with-head-drift provenance while preserving non-stale assessment state
doctor and agent status use shared runner readiness state
live-run invocation adapter command templates survive validation
report packets and review prompt inputs preserve report artifacts, compare artifacts, and comparison questions for reviewer grounding
this supports but does not fully satisfy the named-adapter compare-report persistence claim
